items.title: Pseudo-classes and pseudo-elements: reject the invalid model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling misconception? If an interface works on :hover, it automatically works for keyboard and touch users.
hints: Compare both options with platform semantics and observable user or browser evidence.

feedback.correct: Correct. Design hover, focus, active, disabled, and touch behavior separately, with a visible :focus-visible state.
feedback.incorrect: That preserves the misconception. Design hover, focus, active, disabled, and touch behavior separately, with a visible :focus-visible state.

criteria.description: Selects the source-linked CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling correction rather than the documented misconception.

options.text: Design hover, focus, active, disabled, and touch behavior separately, with a visible :focus-visible state.

items.title: Pseudo-classes and pseudo-elements: verified implementation facet
items.prompt: Style interaction, structural and generated parts while preserving native behavior. Build a bounded artifact demonstrating focus-visible state without replacing native behavior; the locked browser verifier checks observable structure, behavior, or computed presentation.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted authoring draft. Runtime evidence does not replace independent human standards, pedagogy, accessibility, answer-oracle, and formal-exam review.

hints: Implement the smallest artifact that satisfies the declared observable contract, then check the visible case before considering the hidden boundary.

feedback.correct: The locked authoring verifier observed the declared bounded facet in both visible and hidden cases.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic authoring check failed; inspect the exact artifact, interaction state, viewport, and observable result without assuming hidden-case details.

criteria.description: Passes the visible and hidden deterministic checks for the declared implementation facet in its pinned authoring environment.

privateAuthorNotes: This code item and its oracle are AI-assisted draft material with reviewer=null and examEligibility=false.
Browser-verifier items are authoring evidence only and must never be routed to the official untrusted-code runner or formal exam pipeline.
items.starterCode: /* TODO: implement the declared bounded CSS facet. */

answer.referenceSolution: .panel > h2 { margin-block: 0; } .action:hover, .action:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid rgb(0 80 180); } .copy::first-letter { font-weight: 700; }
answer.explanation: The reference is a bounded css artifact for focus-visible state without replacing native behavior. Passing proves only the declared observable facet, not holistic mastery or human accessibility approval.
